sentences.text: The cycle and instret counters serve to support user mode self-profiling usages, wherein a user can read the counter(s) twice and compute the delta(s) to evaluate user software performance and behavior.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction

sentences.text: By default, these counters are not filtered by privilege mode, and thus they continue to increment while traps (e.g., page faults or interrupts) to more privileged code are handled.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction

sentences.text: It introduces unpredictable noise to the counter values observed by the user. * It leaks information about privileged software execution to user mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction

sentences.text: Smcntrpmf remedies these issues by introducing privilege mode filtering for the cycle and instret counters.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction

sentences.text: mcyclecfg and minstretcfg are 64-bit registers that configure privilege mode filtering for the cycle and instret counters, respectively.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > CSRs > Machine Counter Configuration (`mcyclecfg`, `minstretcfg`) Registers

sentences.text: [cols="^1,^1,^1,^1,^1,^1,^5",stripes=even,options="header"] | |63 |62 |61 |60 |59 |58 |57:0 |0 |MINH |SINH |UINH |VSINH |VUINH |WPRI |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > CSRs > Machine Counter Configuration (`mcyclecfg`, `minstretcfg`) Registers

sentences.text: [cols="15%,85%",options="header"] | | Field | Description | MINH |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > CSRs > Machine Counter Configuration (`mcyclecfg`, `minstretcfg`) Registers

sentences.text: If set, then counting of events in M-mode is inhibited | SINH |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > CSRs > Machine Counter Configuration (`mcyclecfg`, `minstretcfg`) Registers

sentences.text: If set, then counting of events in S/HS-mode is inhibited | UINH |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > CSRs > Machine Counter Configuration (`mcyclecfg`, `minstretcfg`) Registers

sentences.text: If set, then counting of events in U-mode is inhibited | VSINH |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > CSRs > Machine Counter Configuration (`mcyclecfg`, `minstretcfg`) Registers

sentences.text: If set, then counting of events in VS-mode is inhibited | VUINH |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > CSRs > Machine Counter Configuration (`mcyclecfg`, `minstretcfg`) Registers

sentences.text: If set, then counting of events in VU-mode is inhibited |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > CSRs > Machine Counter Configuration (`mcyclecfg`, `minstretcfg`) Registers

sentences.text: When all xINH bits are zero, event counting is enabled in all modes.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > CSRs > Machine Counter Configuration (`mcyclecfg`, `minstretcfg`) Registers

sentences.text: For each bit in 61:58, if the associated privilege mode is not implemented, the bit is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > CSRs > Machine Counter Configuration (`mcyclecfg`, `minstretcfg`) Registers

sentences.text: For RV32, bits 63:32 of mcyclecfg can be accessed via the mcyclecfgh CSR, and bits 63:32 of minstretcfg can be accessed via the minstretcfgh CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > CSRs > Machine Counter Configuration (`mcyclecfg`, `minstretcfg`) Registers

sentences.text: The content of these registers may be accessible from Supervisor level if the Smcdeleg/Ssccfg extensions are implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > CSRs > Machine Counter Configuration (`mcyclecfg`, `minstretcfg`) Registers

sentences.text: The fundamental behavior of cycle and instret is modified in that counting does not occur while executing in an inhibited privilege mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Counter Behavior

sentences.text: Further, the following defines how transitions between a non-inhibited privilege mode and an inhibited privilege mode are counted.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Counter Behavior

sentences.text: The cycle counter will simply count CPU cycles while the CPU is in a non-inhibited privilege mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Counter Behavior

sentences.text: Mode transition operations (traps and trap returns) may take multiple clock cycles, and the change of privilege mode may be reported as occurring in any one of those cycles (possibly different for each occurrence of a trap or trap return).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Counter Behavior

sentences.text: For the instret counter, most instructions do not affect mode transitions, so for those the behavior is clear: instructions that retire in a non-inhibited mode increment instret, and instructions that retire in an inhibited mode do not.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Counter Behavior

sentences.text: There are two types of instructions that can affect a privilege mode change: instructions that cause synchronous exceptions to a more privileged mode, and xRET instructions that return to a less privileged mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Counter Behavior

sentences.text: The former are not considered to retire, and hence do not increment instret.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Counter Behavior

sentences.text: The latter do retire, and should increment instret only if the originating privilege mode is not inhibited.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smcntrpmf" Cycle and Instret Privilege Mode Filtering, Version 1.0 > Introduction > Counter Behavior
